New Initiative Exploration Pipeline 0 open questions · refined

Each thinking hat researches each step in its own clean context — fanned out N×M and driven by a Workflow.

“When I start a new goal, I want each hat's take on each step researched in its own clean context — no cross-priming — still synthesized into opinionated per-step playbooks, and surfaced as a polished HTML report I can read and annotate anywhere in Diecast, including an explicit 90/10 laziest-path-to-most-of-the-value hat.”

The move
N × M isolated agents

Every (step, hat) cell gets its own clean-context researcher — ending the single-context priming that waters down contrarian and first-principles takes.

New hat
90/10 → M = 8

The “laziest path to ~90% of the value” idea is promoted from a buried sub-bullet to its own always-on hat.

Scope guard
Additive, parallel

Markdown artifacts and the per-step synthesizer are untouched; HTML is layered on top and the Workflow ships beside cast-explore.

01 · The core move

What we're building and the job it chases

Exploration's research layer is being re-architected so every (step, hat) cell is researched by its own clean-context agent, fanned across N steps × M hats and orchestrated by a deterministic Claude Workflow — because today all hats research inside one per-step context, where “earlier angles bias later ones” and the contrarian and first-principles takes get watered down. The human-in-loop Phase 1 finishes first and hands the approved steps + hat-matrix to the Workflow as args.
US1P1 · user story

Isolated N×M research fan-out

Each (step, hat) is researched by its own clean-context agent, so hats never cross-prime and every perspective stays sharp. Two hat-agent prompts for the same step share no hat-specific framing, and no agent sees another hat's output before producing its own.

US2P1 · user story

Deterministic Workflow orchestration

The autonomous research + synthesis phase is driven by a Workflow script — fan-out and the synthesis barrier are deterministic, replacing hand-rolled child delegation.

US11P2 · user story

Interactive Phase 1 stays with the main agent

Intent, decomposition, step approval, and hat-matrix computation all happen before the Workflow launches, so the human-in-loop decisions precede the non-interactive run. The approved steps and the computed hat-matrix pass in as Workflow args.

FR-001requirement

Workflow is the autonomous-phase entrypoint

The starter-exploration entrypoint launches a Workflow for the autonomous phase — not a child-delegation orchestrator.

FR-002requirement

One agent per applicable cell

Research fan-out is N steps × Mapplicable(step) hats, and each cell runs as a separate agent.

FR-003requirement

Prompt isolation = angle independence

Each hat-agent prompt contains only its own step and its own hat's framing — the structural guarantee that angles stay independent.

FR-004requirement

A lean single-hat researcher agent

A new lean single-hat researcher agent, parameterized by hat, becomes the per-cell worker — reusing only the resilient web-fetch / browser protocol from today's researcher.

FR-014requirement

Approval precedes launch

Interactive decomposition and approval precede the Workflow launch; the approved steps and the hat-matrix are passed as Workflow args.

FR-015requirement

Concurrency cap

The Workflow respects a concurrency cap of min(16, cores−2); excess cells queue rather than overrun the host.

SC-001success criterion

One note per applicable hat per step

Each step has exactly one research note per applicable hat — verified by counting research/*.ai.md against the sum of Mapplicable(step).

SC-002success criterion

No prompt leaks another hat

No hat-agent prompt contains another hat's content — verified by prompt inspection.

02 · The new hat

Promoting 90/10 to a first-class hat — and gating the matrix

The 90/10 solution becomes its own always-on, clean-context hat (Mtotal = 8), and the 80/20 sub-notion is carved out of the First Principles research hat so each angle stays clean. Relevance gating computes Mapplicable(step) at Phase 1 so only applicable hats run per step — but Contrarian, First Principles, and 90/10 are never gated out, and the decomposer's 4-lens set is untouched.
The 8 hats (M_total = 8)
#HatGenerative questionGating
1Expert PractitionerHow do the world's best people / orgs do this?gateable
2Tool / Product LandscapeBest tools — how do they really compare?gateable
3AI-Native / InnovationWhat's newly possible with AI that wasn't 2 years ago?gateable
4Community WisdomWhat do practitioners who've actually done this say?gateable
5Framework / MethodologyWhat structured approaches exist?gateable
6ContrarianWhat does the majority get wrong?always-on
7First PrinciplesFrom scratch, physics-only — and what IS the value here?always-on
890/10 Solution · NEWWhat gets ~90% of this step's value for ~10% of the effort?always-on

“Accomplish 90% of what you want with only 10% of the work / effort / time… a 90% solution available right away beats a 100% solution that takes ages.” — the hat is a builder proposing the laziest viable cut, accepting the step's value as given, not an auditor. It flags disqualified cuts: hard-10%-is-the-moat, regulated / trust-critical, or irreversible one-way doors.

vs First Principles

First Principles re-litigates what the value is and may reframe or shrink the step; 90/10 never re-opens the goal — it finds the cheapest path to the given value.

vs Contrarian

Contrarian runs the broad adversarial failure-hunt; 90/10 proposes a cut and self-checks only enough to keep that cut safe.

US3P1 · user story

First-class 90/10 hat

A dedicated 90/10 hat gives every step a clean-context deep dive on the laziest-high-value path instead of a buried one-liner. Test: a …-90-10.ai.md note exists per step, and the First Principles note contains no 80/20 content.

US4P2 · user story

Relevance-gated matrix

Only applicable hats run per step, so cost stays controlled and low-signal notes don't bloat synthesis. For a pure-strategy step, gateable hats are absent while the always-on hats are still present.

FR-005requirement

90/10 in, 80/20 out

Mtotal = 8 includes the new 90/10 hat, and the 80/20 sub-notion is removed from the First Principles research hat.

FR-006requirement

Decomposer lenses unchanged

The decomposer's 4-lens set — including its “10x” lens — is unchanged; that is a decomposition-time device, not a research hat.

FR-007requirement

Hat-matrix gating with locked always-on hats

Relevance gating computes Mapplicable(step) at interactive Phase 1 from each step's type / tags; Contrarian, First Principles, and 90/10 are never gated out. The output is a hat-matrix (step → hats) passed as a Workflow arg, mirroring today's code-exploration gating.

SC-003success criterion

90/10 present, First Principles clean

A 90/10 note exists for every step, and the First Principles note has no 80/20 content — verified by file inspection.

03 · What stays the same

The playbook deliverable and md substrate stay intact

Only the research layer changes. The per-step synthesizer is preserved — one synthesizer per step producing exactly one opinionated playbook — so the actionable deliverable is unchanged. Every existing markdown artifact is still written at its current path and shape, a hard compatibility contract with cast-high-level-planner; HTML is additive, never a replacement.
US5P2 · user story

Per-step synthesis unchanged

The existing per-step synthesizer is preserved — one synthesizer per step → one opinionated playbook — so the actionable-playbook deliverable is unchanged; only the research layer gains the hat dimension. Test: exactly one playbook file per step, as today.

US6P2 · user story

Markdown substrate preserved

All existing md artifacts are still produced at existing paths so cast-high-level-planner consumes them unchanged; HTML is additive, not a replacement.

FR-008requirement

Synthesis is unchanged

Per-step synthesis stays exactly as today: one synthesizer per step → one playbook.

FR-009requirement

Md artifacts at existing paths

All existing markdown artifacts are still produced, rooted at goals/{slug}/: exploration/research/{NN}-{step}-{hat}.ai.md, exploration/playbooks/{NN}-{step}.ai.md, and exploration/summary.ai.md.

SC-004success criterion

One playbook per step

One opinionated playbook per step is still produced — verified by counting playbooks/*.ai.md against N.

04 · The new surface

A Diecast-wide HTML render and commenting surface

Dual md/html rendering plus inline commenting becomes a Diecast-wide capability. A reusable trio — a WHAT content agent (no HTML), a HOW presentation agent (one self-contained bespoke doc), and a render-checker — turns artifacts into polished HTML, modeled on the v3 requirements renderer. The viewer renders it via iframe/srcdoc right where markdown shows today, and any HTML artifact becomes selectable-and-commentable. Exploration is consumer #1; refined-requirements is consumer #2.
US7P1 · user story

General WHAT/HOW HTML render capability

A content agent (decides what each section conveys, emits no HTML) + a presentation agent (renders bespoke HTML) + a render-checker let any artifact become a polished HTML view, modeled on the v3 requirements renderer (render_job_service.py).

US8P1 · user story

Dual md/html artifact viewer

The artifact viewer renders both .md (as today) and .html (via iframe/srcdoc) in the same phase-tab surface where markdown shows now — so polished HTML appears in place without leaving the viewer.

US9P1 · user story

Diecast-wide commenting

A user can select text in any HTML artifact and leave a comment. Comments use the /cast-comment-html {quoted_text, section_hint, body} shape and the same-door re-anchor API.

US10P2 · user story

refined-requirements HTML in-viewer

The refined-requirements render — today exiled to /goals/{slug}/render — is surfaced in the dual viewer so HTML + commenting is uniform across Diecast. Requirements is consumer #2; exploration is consumer #1.

FR-010requirement

WHAT + HOW + render-checker trio

A WHAT agent (content) + HOW agent (HTML) + render-checker produce polished HTML, tool-free, gated by Python checks mirroring their contracts. Exploration HTML lands at goals/{slug}/exploration/exploration.html (atomic write, served-by stamp), modeled on render_job_service.py.

FR-011requirement

Dual viewer + gate/glob extension

The artifact viewer renders .html (iframe/srcdoc) alongside .md; the markdown-only gate (api_artifacts.py:52) and the phase-tab globs (api_goals.py:~422) are extended to allow .html.

FR-012requirement

Commentable HTML via same-door API

HTML artifacts are servable to /cast-comment-html; comments feed the same-door re-anchor API via verbatim-substring relocation this round (stable anchor-ids deferred).

FR-013requirement

Requirements as consumer #2

The refined-requirements HTML render is reachable in the dual viewer, generalizing the capability beyond a one-off.

FR-017requirement

4-criteria render-checker rubric

The exploration render-checker grades four criteria: (1) every applicable hat is visible per step; (2) the per-step opinionated POV is legible at the zero-click surface; (3) hat perspectives stay distinct, not prematurely blended; (4) visual quality is real, not generic AI-slop. It mirrors the requirements render-checker's comprehension + visual structure, adapted to the distinct-hats requirement.

SC-005success criterion

Polished HTML passes the checker

A polished exploration HTML renders and passes the render-checker's 4 criteria — verified by the checker verdict.

SC-006success criterion

Both views in the phase tab

The viewer shows both the .html report and the .md artifacts in the phase tab — verified by UI check.

SC-007success criterion

Selection → comment, end to end

Selecting text in any HTML artifact yields a comment via the same-door API — verified end-to-end.

SC-008success criterion

Requirements HTML in the dual viewer

refined-requirements HTML is viewable in the dual viewer — verified by UI check.

05 · Resilience

One bad cell never sinks the run

Failure is isolated per cell. A failed hat-agent is logged and its cell dropped to null, and the step's playbook still synthesizes from the surviving hats. Combined with the deterministic Workflow, a full autonomous exploration completes without manual intervention once the decomposition is approved.
US12P3 · user story

Failure isolation

A failed hat-agent drops that cell to null (logged) so one bad cell never sinks the step or the run; the playbook synthesizes from the surviving hats. Test: with one hat-agent forced to fail, the run completes and the step's playbook is still produced.

FR-016requirement

Log, null-drop, continue

A failed hat-agent is logged and drops its cell to null without sinking the step or the run.

SC-009success criterion

Hands-off completion after approval

A full exploration completes without manual intervention after step approval — verified by a run.

06 · The fence

Deliberately out of scope

This is a parallel addition, not a migration. cast-explore is not retired — the Workflow ships alongside it and the user merges later. Markdown is not replaced, and neither the per-step synthesis model nor the decomposer's lens set changes.
  • ×Rewriting cast-high-level-planner or any downstream consumer; changing the summary.ai.md format.
  • ×Adding CEO/Viability, Security/Risk, or Eng-Pragmatism hats — 90/10 only this round.
  • ×A stable anchor-id scheme for HTML comments — deferred; verbatim-substring relocation this round.
  • ×Changing the per-step synthesis model or the decomposer's lens set.
  • ×Replacing markdown with HTML — HTML is additive visualization.
  • ×Retiring cast-explore / a big-bang migration — the Workflow ships in parallel; the user merges later.
  • ×Multi-goal orchestration.
07 · Settled

What's decided, and what's still open

✓ Zero open questions — every ambiguity was resolved during refinement, and the 4-criteria render-checker rubric is pinned.
ChoseOverBecause
New goal as homereuse existing / standalone docdistinct, sizable multi-pillar feature
new_initiative familyrefactor_migrationlarge new design surface warrants full PRD depth
Relevance-gated matrixfull Cartesian / tunable knobcost control; matches code-exploration gating precedent
Synthesis unchanged (multi-agent split)perspectives-only / origin-pure“split between diff agents is right” — only research isolation changes
90/10 only (M=8); gstack = techniques onlyadd CEO/Security/Eng hats; gstack ethosavoid dilution — never the boil-the-ocean ethos or review/score principles, only techniques
Carve 80/20 out of First Principlesleave it embeddedclean angle-independence; 90/10 gets its own clean-context depth
Dual md/html, in-viewer (iframe/srcdoc), Diecast-widehtml-only / md-only / its own /render page“md files will still be produced… html on top… part of entire diecast,” shown where md is now
Verbatim-substring relocation this roundstable anchor-ids nowproven cast-comment-html path; defer ids
Workflow ships in parallel to cast-explorereplace the autonomous phase outright“have it in parallel — I will take care of merging later”
New lean single-hat agentrefactor cast-web-researcher to dual-modedon't carry forward the 7-in-one-context structure we're retiring
Lock the render-checker rubric now (4 criteria)defer to cast-detailed-planenough known from the requirements checker; closes the only open item
Exploration Pipeline · N×M Workflow + 90/10 Hat + Diecast HTML Surface — refined requirements, new_initiative · 0 open questions. Markdown artifacts remain the compatibility contract; this HTML view is additive.
